scope_note:
QA Wall-Sun-Sun Depth Zero cert [420]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) pp. 525-532 doi:10.2307/2309169 (rank of apparition alpha(p); original WSS conjecture: no prime satisfies p^2|F_{p-(5/p)}); Sun, Z.-H. and Sun, Z.-W. (1992) 'Fibonacci and Lucas congruences and their applications' Acta Arithmetica 61 pp. 119-129 (LTE for Fibonacci: v_p(F_{k*alpha})=v_p(F_alpha)+v_p(k) for p|F_alpha, p not-divides k; formal WSS definition); McIntosh, R.J. and Roettger, E.L. (2007) 'A search for Fibonacci-Wieferich and Wolstenholme primes' Mathematics of Computation 76(260) pp. 2087-2094 doi:10.1090/S0025-5718-07-01955-2 (exhaustive search: no WSS prime < 9.7*10^14). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): A prime p is a Wall-Sun-Sun prime iff delta(p)=0 (cert [419] defines delta(p)=F_{alpha(p)}/p mod p). The equivalence with the classical formulation p^2|F_{p-(5/p)} holds for all odd primes p!=5, established in three steps: (STEP 1 — LTE) v_p(F_{k*alpha})=v_p(F_alpha)+v_p(k) for p odd, p|F_alpha, p not-divides k; this is the Lifting-the-Exponent identity for Fibonacci sequences (Sun-Sun 1992); (STEP 2 — r bound) setting r=(p-(5/p))/alpha(p), the analytic bound r

=2 for all primes p>=3); therefore p does not divide r, so v_p(r)=0; (STEP 3 — equivalence) combining: v_p(F_{p-(5/p)})=v_p(F_{r*alpha})=v_p(F_alpha)+v_p(r)=v_p(F_alpha)+0=v_p(F_alpha); therefore p^2|F_{p-(5/p)} iff v_p(F_{alpha(p)})>=2 iff delta(p)=0. CHECKS: (C1) LTE IDENTITY: verified numerically for 59 cases across 10 test primes {5,11,19,29,31,41,71,89,101,149}; includes k not-divides p (v_p unchanged) and k=p (v_p increments by 1); falsifiable: any (p,k) pair where v_p(F_{k*alpha}) != v_p(F_alpha)+v_p(k) refutes; (C2) CLASSICAL EQUIVALENCE: for all 93 odd primes !=5 in [3,500]: (p^2|F_{p-(5/p)}) iff (p^2|F_{alpha(p)}) — both False for every prime in range (no WSS prime found); falsifiable: any prime where the two conditions disagree refutes; (C3) NO WSS PRIME <= 500000: direct fast-doubling computation (O(log p) per prime) over 41536 odd primes !=5 up to 500000; F_{p-(5/p)} mod p^2 != 0 for all; known record extends to 9.7*10^14 (McIntosh-Roettger 2007); (C4) r COPRIME TO p: for all 93 odd primes !=5 in [3,500]: r=(p-(5/p))/alpha(p) satisfies r

[417] delta(p)=F_{alpha}/p mod p defined -> [418] alpha parity via Cassini -> [419] delta=1 census -> [420] delta=0 is WSS open conjecture. All integer arithmetic, no float, Theorem NT. Derived 2026-06-14.
